description: Baseline CacheIR GetBoundName IC returned the wrong `this` object across a security boundary. CVE-2024-3852 (sec-high, MFSA 2024-18, Firefox 125). When a getter was installed on the global-lexical binding and invoked through the Baseline GetBoundName inline cache, the IC exposed the bare inner global object as `this` instead of the WindowProxy. Regression from Bug 1805199 (getters on global). Calling a receiver-sensitive method (e.g., the JS shell builtin `newString`) on the bare global then segfaults because the IC trusted the wrong receiver shape. The fix (commit 86083f35c4dc832565464cca79f6224821a985e0 by Iain Ireland, 2024-03-21, r=jandem, Phabricator D204130) simplifies GetBoundName ICs: IsCacheableGetPropCall now gains an op parameter and returns NativeGetPropKind::None for JSOp::GetBoundName, preventing the IC from attaching a cached getter path that can smuggle the bare global. Follow-up commit 83c1f27889e88fb90d6eb60846f7c57820519529 adds js/src/jit-test/tests/cacheir/bug1883542.js and a MOZ_ASSERT(!IsWindow(nobj)). Reporter: Logan Stratton (co-credit: Andrew Kramer).

fixes.description: Bug 1883542: Add test and assertion r=jandem
